question.text: Low Frequency Algo in commodity markets is defined as:

answer.text: An algorithm with order frequency of 10 OPS (Orders Per Second) or less
An algorithm that trades once per day
An algorithm with more than 10 OPS
Any algorithm that uses limit orders only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Low Frequency Algo ≤ 10 OPS. High Frequency Algo > 10 OPS. Each category has different registration and monitoring requirements. **The Trap:** 'Low frequency' doesn't mean once per day. > 10 OPS is High Frequency. Order type doesn't define frequency. **Cross-Exam Context:** Algo classification is relevant to exchange regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Low Frequency Algos get a generic identifier; High Frequency Algos need individual registration. Algo providers for HFT must register as Research Analysts with SEBI.
